source-discipline.reason: All five batch-3 probes performed live this session with HTTP responses recorded as observed: SE Boverket 3-URL 404; CA RHFAC two-hop home→/rhfac-v40 200 75KB; SG BCA 403; INT ISO multiple URL 404+wrong-match; INT ASPECTSS Archnet 429 + DCU 404 + Emerald 403 + CrossRef wrong-year. Eligibility delta arithmetic reconciled explicitly: 221 + 4 batch1 + 0 batch2 - 1 dedup + 1 batch3 = 225, not papered over.

best-practice-synthesis-routing.reason: DR amendment is methodology; Sonnet scope.

evidence-verification-gate-for-synthesis.reason: Batch 3 added 1 strict VERIFIED to rule #10 eligible pool. Cumulative across 3 batches: 5 of 14 (36%) cleared gate, 6 of 14 (43%) owner-actionable via paywall or human, 3 of 14 (21%) V2-blocked. The middle-cohort yield validates the population-yield projection from batch 2's amendment within tolerance, sharpening the §6 step 5 promotion-to-skill calibration. Also expanded test_db_integrity B01 allowlist to match V1 §4 state-machine spec rather than only data observed.

bias_direction: The cumulative 36% gate-clear rate falls within the projected 25-40% range from batch 2 amendment. Batch 3 was deliberately structured to test mid-difficulty cases — that increases the risk of confirmation bias (picking sites I knew or suspected would work, e.g., rickhansen.com, which I had not actually probed before but which had high prior probability of being static-EN-NGO). The honest accounting in the changelog includes the dedup absorption (REF-00047 deleted) that masked one batch-3 gain, rather than reporting the gross +1 as if it were a clean net gain.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: An outside reviewer could argue that batch 3 still oversamples favorable cases: 1 of 5 cleared the gate, but the 4 non-clearing rows split into 3 actionable lanes (IS-PAYWALL, NEEDS-HUMAN, DEFERRED-V2-AUTOMATED), which makes them look more tractable than they are. NEEDS-HUMAN in particular assumes the owner will follow up on different-IP fetches, but there are 2 such rows now and no demonstrated cycle of one closing. The IS-PAYWALL queue (4 rows) also has no demonstrated end-to-end cycle. Until those cycles close, the 'owner-actionable' framing is theoretical, not validated. The DR §6 step 5 third requirement (≥1 IS-PAYWALL purchase cycle) remains unmet and the recommendation to defer skill promotion until then is the correct call.
